spec_match.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the concept uses real overflow:hidden + white-space:nowrap + text-overflow:ellipsis targets. Focusing and selecting the long task kept computed text-overflow as ellipsis while the active overflowing state was reported for Chrome 148 interaction rendering.

interactive.rationale: The page now has controls for focusing a long task, selecting the hidden tail, switching to short no-overflow tasks, restoring long tasks, and blurring the active task.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and comparison text describe the exact CSS behaviour: ellipsis at rest, browser-managed clipping while focused or selected, and restoration on blur.

edge_cases.rationale: The Short text edge control exercises the no-overflow case and the probe reports that no ellipsis interaction is needed when text fits.

references.rationale: A labelled references section links to ChromeStatus feature 5146265241387008, CSS Overflow 3 ellipsis interaction, and the Chrome 148 beta note.

design_system.rationale: The added controls and probe use shared CSS variables, and button background overrides include explicit text colour overrides.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome/151 verified focus and selection of the long task as active overflowing ellipsis states, the short-task edge as non-overflowing, all reference links present, and no console or network errors. The feature conformance route reported 5 PASS / 0 FAIL.
